canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, SQuAD 1.1 is consistently defined as a reading comprehension benchmark. The definitions are in agreement, describing it as a dataset based on Wikipedia articles where the answer to every question must be a direct text span from the provided passage. The primary and explicit use of this instrument is to measure the behavior of "Question answering." Evidence from multiple papers shows it is used for "QA tasks," with one source specifying its questions as "single-hop." The instrument is referred to as both "SQuAD v1.1" and "SQuAD v1" in the source materials, though the underlying description of its structure and purpose remains consistent.
papers: Bridging the Language Gaps in Large Language Models with Inference-Time Cross-Lingual Intervention
Innovative Image Fraud Detection with Cross-Sample Anomaly Analysis: The Power ofLLMs
Towards a More Generalized Approach in Open Relation Extraction
Who Writes What: Unveiling the Impact of Author Roles onAI-generated Text Detection

definitions.text: The Stanford Question Answering Dataset version 1.1, a reading comprehension benchmark where answers are text spans from a given Wikipedia article.
definitions.paper: Bridging the Language Gaps in Large Language Models with Inference-Time Cross-Lingual Intervention

definitions.text: The Stanford Question Answering Dataset (SQuAD) v1 is a reading comprehension benchmark where the answer to every question is a span of text from a given Wikipedia article.
definitions.paper: Innovative Image Fraud Detection with Cross-Sample Anomaly Analysis: The Power ofLLMs

relationships.paper: Accelerating Blockwise Parallel Language Models with Draft Refinement
relationships.evidence: We used SQuAD v1.1 (Rajpurkar et al., 2016) for QA tasks (Section 4.1)

neurips24: Accelerating Blockwise Parallel Language Models with Draft Refinement
icml25: A Lens into Interpretable Transformer Mistakes via Semantic Dependency
